title: Trial workflow — narrative examples
description: Concrete examples of the trial lifecycle for each trial_type + trialist_type combination. Consumed by SDUI documentation panels + test fixtures. Illustrates the atomic conversion path + rejection defence-record path + anonymous PII redaction schedule.

transitions.completed_to_converted: coach's decision — atomic conversion (TeamMember row created same transaction)
transitions.scheduled_or_in_progress_to_cancelled: voluntary cancel by trialist or admin

examples.name: Individual trial — athlete → converted
narrative: 2026-06-01 09:00 — Parent schedules a trial for Athlete Aisha (age 9). trialist_type='athlete', trialist_id='ath_...'. trial_type='individual', session_count=1, starts_at=2026-06-08T16:00, ends_at=2026-06-08T17:00.
TrialScheduled fires. NotifyTrialistPreTrialJob scheduled at 24h + 1h.
2026-06-07 16:00 — 24h reminder dispatched.
2026-06-08 15:00 — 1h reminder dispatched.
2026-06-08 16:00 — Coach marks status='in_progress'. Trial session runs.
2026-06-08 17:00 — Coach marks status='completed' + writes evaluation_notes.
2026-06-09 10:00 — Coach clicks convert. Body: { notes: 'Excellent basics + attitude. Add to U-10 squad.', role_on_team: 'player', jersey_number: 12 }.
TrialObserver runs the atomic conversion INSIDE a transaction: UPDATE team_trials SET status='converted' + INSERT team_members (member_type='athlete', member_id='ath_...').
TrialConvertedToMember + TeamMemberJoined fire in the same afterCommit.
TrialDecisionNotification (accepted variant) dispatched to Aisha's parent.

examples.name: Individual trial — athlete → rejected
narrative: Same setup as above.
2026-06-09 10:00 — Coach clicks reject. Body: { notes: 'Not meeting the age-appropriate skill benchmarks for U-10 competition. Recommend recreational-tier gym program instead.' }.
TrialObserver refuses if decision_notes is empty (returns TRIAL_DECISION_NOTES_REQUIRED).
status → rejected. TrialRejected event fires.
Audit row written with 3-year retention (anti-discrimination defence record).
TrialDecisionNotification (rejected variant) dispatched — fixed template, does NOT surface decision_notes.
Analytics fires 'Trial Rejected' with team_id + trial_type — NO reason label.

examples.name: Multi-session trial — adult user (gym class) → converted
narrative: 2026-07-01 — User Michael Torres schedules a multi-session trial for a Boxing class. trial_type='multi_session', session_count=3, starts_at=2026-07-06, ends_at=2026-07-13.
Sessions run on the 6th, 8th, 10th, 13th. Coach transitions to in_progress after session 1, completed after session 3.
2026-07-15 — Coach converts. TeamMember created (member_type='user', member_id='usr_...', role_on_team='player'). Michael joins the recurring class.

examples.name: One-day drop-in trial — anonymous walk-in → converted
narrative: 2026-07-20 15:00 — A parent walks in with their 8-year-old asking about swimming lessons. Reception schedules a trial: trialist_type='anonymous', trialist_first_name='James', trialist_last_name='Wilson', trialist_phone='+14155551234', trialist_dob='2018-04-12'. trial_type='one_day', session_count=1, starts_at=2026-07-20T16:00, ends_at=2026-07-20T17:00.
TrialObserver refuses if trialist_first_name or trialist_phone is missing (data-quality mandate).
Coach runs the trial. Marks completed at 17:00 with positive evaluation_notes.
2026-07-20 18:00 — Admin creates an Athlete + User account for James (offline flow — Wave 3 Sports module owns Athlete creation). Then invokes trial:convert with the newly-created athlete_id.
Trial converts. TeamMember created. Anonymous PII fields (trialist_first_name/last_name/phone/dob) migrated into the Athlete row; cleared on the trial.

examples.name: Anonymous trial — rejected → 90-day PII redaction
narrative: Anonymous trial captured at 2026-06-01 for a parent inquiring on behalf of a child. trial_type='one_day', trialist_dob='2015-03-14'.
Coach rejects with decision_notes. Trial in rejected state, decided_at=2026-06-08.

2026-09-06 (90 days later) — RedactAnonymousTrialistProfileJob sweep. Field-level redaction: trialist_first_name = '[REDACTED]', trialist_last_name = '[REDACTED]', trialist_phone = '[REDACTED]', trialist_email = '[REDACTED]', trialist_dob = null.
The trial row survives with decision_notes intact (anti-discrimination defence). Only the anonymous PII sub-fields are redacted per GDPR Art. 5(1)(c).
3 years after decided_at — retention pipeline hard-deletes the row entirely.

examples.name: Trial → no-show
narrative: Trial scheduled for 2026-06-20 15:00. Trialist doesn't turn up.
2026-06-20 15:30 — Coach marks status='no_show'. TrialNoShow fires.
Retention: 1 year (shorter than rejected — no decision was actually made).
PII redaction: 90 days post-decided_at for anonymous trialists.

examples.name: Trial → cancelled by trialist
narrative: Trial scheduled for 2026-07-05 10:00. Trialist calls in 48h before to cancel.
2026-07-03 10:00 — Reception cancels the trial. TrialCancelled fires with cancelled_by=receptionist + cancellation_reason='trialist_requested'.
Retention: 1 year.

examples.name: Atomic conversion race — trial converts but capacity fills first
narrative: Trial completes. Team has capacity=20; 19 active roster rows.
Two coaches simultaneously click convert on two different completed trials for the same team.
First convert: transaction acquires row-lock, inserts TeamMember row 20. Team is now at capacity.
Second convert: transaction acquires row-lock on the OTHER trial, tries to insert TeamMember row 21. CapacityGate refuses. WHOLE TRANSACTION ROLLS BACK.
Trial 2 stays in status='completed' (not 'converted'). Coach 2 sees TRIAL_CONVERSION_CAPACITY_EXCEEDED (402) with 'retry after freeing a slot' guidance.
